title: Stamp User ID on DTP_SIGNUP Table During Registration
description:
When a user signs up and authenticates into DataTools Pro, the system should stamp the `user_id` on the `DTP_SIGNUP` table. This creates a complete audit trail from initial sign-up through OTP verification to user creation.

A `used_by` field was added to the signup model. Changes were deployed to production and tested by creating a new user through the dtpwaqar Salesforce instance.
acceptance_criteria: The user_id is written to the DTP_SIGNUP table when a user completes authentication
The audit trail links sign-up record to OTP verification to user creation
Existing sign-up records without user_id remain unaffected
middleware_changes: Updated the sign-up authentication flow to write the user_id back to the DTP_SIGNUP table upon successful user creation.
data_model_changes: Added `used_by` (user_id) field to the `DTP_SIGNUP` table to track which user account was created from a given sign-up record.
implementation_notes: Implemented and deployed to production. Tested by creating a new user on the dtpwaqar Salesforce instance and verifying the used_by field was populated.
comment_summary: Developer confirmed the used_by field was added, deployed to production, and tested successfully by creating a new user through the dtpwaqar instance.
